methods.description: Collection of site-specific climate and soil data to understand environmental conditions affecting tree growth.

steps.description: The three study sites are: (1) an alpine forest in North Italy (Renon, REN), (2) a lowland forest in Germany (Hainich, HAI) and (3) a boreal forest in North Sweden (Flakaliden, FLA).
steps.searchable_summary: Identify and document study sites for climate data collection

steps.description: Annual temperatures were highest in HAI and lowest in FLA, and the length of the growing season was longest in Germany.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect annual temperature and growing season length data
Document soil conditions at each study site

steps.description: Since the CarboEurope climate data cover only 5 years, additionally long-term meteorological data were used from weather stations.
steps.searchable_summary: Fetch long-term meteorological data from weather stations

methods.description: Collection and analysis of tree core samples to study tree growth patterns and environmental influences.

steps.description: Wood samples were collected by coring one core per tree at breast height with a 5-mm-diameter increment borer.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect tree core samples using increment borer

steps.description: Tree-ring width was measured on all cores using a semiautomatic device LINTAB-III.
Cross-dating of individual cores was carried out based on tree-ring width using the program COFECHA.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform cross-dating of tree cores using COFECHA

steps.description: Absolute values of each tree-ring width were standardized by calculating the ratio of the tree-ring deviation in a certain year and the long-term average.
